perturbation.original_text: Licensee shall have the non-exclusive right and license to develop and distribute ICE AGE 2 Wireless Products during the Term of the Agreement for all Wireless Products set forth in this Paragraph 2(c) except the Game, for which Licensee shall have the exclusive right and license to develop and distribute until December 31, 2006. For the avoidance of doubt, Licensee's right and license to develop and distribute the Game in connection with the Property "ICE AGE 2" shall become non-exclusive after December 31, 2006.
perturbation.changed_text: Licensee shall have the exclusive right and license to develop and distribute ICE AGE 2 Wireless Products during the Term of the Agreement for all Wireless Products set forth in this Paragraph 2(c), including the Game, and this exclusivity shall remain in effect indefinitely. For the avoidance of doubt, Licensee's right and license to develop and distribute the Game in connection with the Property "ICE AGE 2" shall remain exclusive even after December 31, 2006.
perturbation.explanation: The original text grants Glu Mobile exclusive rights to the 'ICE AGE 2' game only until December 31, 2006, after which it becomes non-exclusive. The modified text states that Glu Mobile has exclusive rights to all 'ICE AGE 2' Wireless Products (including the game) indefinitely, even after December 31, 2006. This creates a contradiction regarding the duration of exclusivity for the game and other wireless products. The contradiction occurs in this paragraph.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Licensee shall have the exclusive right and license to develop and distribute ICE AGE 2 Wireless Products during the Term of the Agreement for all Wireless Products set forth in this Paragraph 2(c), including the Game, and this exclusivity shall remain in effect indefinitely. For the avoidance of doubt, Licensee's right and license to develop and distribute the Game in connection with the Property "ICE AGE 2" shall remain exclusive even after December 31, 2006. (A) "ICE AGE 2" RESERVATION OF RIGHTS: Fox hereby reserves the right to develop, publish and/or distribute (or grant a third party the right to develop, publish, and/or distribute) any wireless products or wireless content or any nature which may be derived from the property "ICE AGE 2". Notwithstanding the foregoing, in no event will Fox develop, publish and/or distribute games derived from the Property "ICE AGE 2" prior to January 1, 2007.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the exclusivity terms for ICE AGE 2 wireless products, including the game, making it indefinite. This directly contradicts the 'ICE AGE 2' RESERVATION OF RIGHTS section, which grants Fox the right to develop, publish, and/or distribute any wireless products or wireless content derived from 'ICE AGE 2' after January 1, 2007. The changed text eliminates the timeframe for exclusivity, preventing Fox from exercising their reserved rights. The contradiction is impactful and creates uncertainty about the obligations of the parties.

perturbation.original_text: Fox hereby reserves the right to distribute all Wireless Products through Fox's direct-to-consumer distribution channels, for which Fox shall retain a Distribution Fee as defined in Paragraph 4(a) of this Amendment.
perturbation.changed_text: Fox hereby waives the right to distribute all Wireless Products through Fox's direct-to-consumer distribution channels, relinquishing all claim to a Distribution Fee as defined in Paragraph 4(a) of this Amendment. Licensee shall have sole right to distribute the wireless products. Fox shall not be able to distribute the wireless products.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly reserves Fox's right to distribute Wireless Products through its own channels, retaining a Distribution Fee. The modified text completely reverses this, stating that Fox waives this right and gives the sole rights of distribution to the licensee. This creates a direct in-text contradiction regarding distribution rights which can be seen between Section 4 and Section 4(a).
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) DISTRIBUTION FEE FOR DIRECT-TO-CONSUMER DISTRIBUTION: Should Fox or its assigns choose to distribute the Wireless Products through Fox's and its assigns' direct-to-consumer distribution channels, Fox or its assigns shall deduct a percentage of Fox's Gross Receipts (defined as monies received by or credited to Fox or its assigns from Fox's or its assigns' direct-to-consumer distribution channels, for the download of the Wireless Products by end users, or the sale or download of Wireless Products to end users) collected from the sale of such Wireless Products ("Distribution Fee") in the amount of ***** percent (*****%) of Fox's Gross Receipts. After Fox or its assigns deducts its Distribution Fee, it shall remit the remainder to Licensee and Licensee shall pay to Fox or its assigns or such other party as Fox or its assigns may designate in writing, Royalties in the amounts set forth in the Agreement or this Amendment.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text and contradicted text both state Fox can distribute the Wireless Products and collect fees. The changed text explicitly waives Fox's right to distribute the products and collect distribution fees, which is the subject of the contradicted text. This creates a clear contradiction as the contradicted text discusses the percentage of fees Fox collects, while the changed text says it has no right to do so.
